title: astragalus arrectus var. palousensis
intro: Astragalus arrectus var. palousensis, also known as Palouse milkvetch, is a plant species in the pea family that is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of Palouse milkvetch are attributed to its ability to enhance immune function, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture or tea, and its roots, leaves, and stems can be used in herbal preparations to promote overall well-being.
